folio.label: 170r
folio.l: chastisynge of the trespassours

¢ And ©et seye I more , æ rigút
as a singuler ¹sone synnetú , in takyngö vengeau~ce of anoæä
man ,

rigút so synnetú the Iuge , If he do no vengeau~ce of he~
æ it han disserued

¢ For Senek seitú ; thus , æ maister he seitú
is god , p p æ prouetú shrewes

¢ And Cassidore seitú , A man dre-
detú to do outges , whan he woot & knowetú , æ it displesetú to
the Iuges & souereyns ,,

And another seitú ; The Iuge æ dre-
detú to do rigút , maketú men shrewes

but æei
beren it , to puysshepunysshepunysshe the shrewes & mysdoers , & for to defende
the goode men

¢ If ©e wyln tha~ne take vengeance of ©oure
enemys , ©e shuln retourne or haue ©oure recours , to æe Iuge
æ hatú the Iurisdiccou~ vp on he~ ,

and he shal punysshe he~ , as
the lawe axetú & requyretú

¢ A quod Melibe ; This vengeau~ce
liketú me no thyngö ,

I bithenke me now & take hede , how for-
tune hatú norisshed me fro my childhode , & hatú holpen me
to passe many a strongö paas

¢ Now wol I assayen hire trow-
ynge , w goddes help , æ she shal helpe me , my shame for
to venge

¢ Certes q¶ Pèdence , if ©e wol werke by my cou~seil
©e shuln nat assaie fortune by no wey ,

ne ©e ne shuln nat
lene or bowe vn to hire , after the word of Senek

¢ For thynges
æ ben folily doon , And tho æ ben doon in hope of fortune , sh-
uln neuäe come to good ende

trustetú nat in hire , for she
nys nat stedefast ne stable ,

for whan æè trowest to be moost
syker & seure of hire helpe , she wol faile & deceyue the

¢ And
where as ©e seyn , æ fortune hatú norisshed ©ow , fro ©owre
childhod ,

I seye , in so muchel , shuln ©e the lasse , truste in hire
and in hire wyt

¢ For Senek seitú ; what ma~ æ is norisshed
by fortune , she maketú hi~ a greet fool

¢ Now tha~ne , syn
©e desire , & axe vengeau~ce , & the vengeau~ce æ is don , after
the lawe , & bifore the Iuge , liketú ©ow nat ,

And the ven-
geau~ce æ is doon in hope of fortune , is ¹ilous & vncerteyn ,

tha~ne haue ©e noon oæä remedie , but for to haue ©oure recours
vn to the souereigne Iuge , æ vengetú alle vyleynyes & wron-
ges ,

& he shal venge ©ow , aft ä æ hi~ self witnessetú ¢ Where
as he seitú ,
leuetú the vengeau~ce to me , & I shal do it ,,

Me-
libeus answerde , If I ne venge me nat of the vyleynye , æ
men han don to me ,
I sompne or warne he~ , æ han doon to
